design_philosophy.core_loop: Place randomly-dispensed pipe pieces on a grid to form an unbroken pipeline of sufficient length before the flow starts, then route the flow through your pipeline to reach the goal
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time pressure: you must plan ahead with pieces you cannot rotate or control their order, then execute correctly once the flow begins. Timer creates escalating urgency across levels
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Quick-thinking puzzle solver who can visualize spatial solutions and adapt to constraints. Brain-twitch gameplay emphasizing forward-planning under pressure
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pipe Dream enforces strict constraints (no rotation, no control over piece order, mandatory time delay before flow) that force players into spatial reasoning and risk assessment rather than pure reflexes. The game trusts players to understand the puzzle rather than hand-holding them

difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows resuming from specific levels after bonus rounds
difficulty.checkpointing: Bonus rounds after every fourth level serve as checkpoints; passwords allow mid-game resumption
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: players can choose to replace pieces before flow starts (with score penalty) or accept risk of failure. Can attempt to exceed minimum requirements for bonus points. No difficulty select mentioned

progression.upgrade_system: Escalating challenge mechanics: higher minimum pipeline lengths, shorter delays before flow starts, higher flow speeds, pre-placed obstacles, directional pipe sections, wrap-around edges, reservoirs that give time bonuses, bonus pipes for extra points
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression to next level contingent on meeting minimum pipeline length requirement. Bonus rounds unlock every fourth level and award passwords for non-sequential level access
replayability_hooks: Score optimization: players can attempt to exceed minimum length for bonus points
Four-way intersection bonus: routing flow through intersections five times awards large bonus
Password system enables targeted practice on specific difficulty tiers

world_building_techniques: Thematic visual language: green slime ('flooz'/'goo') as metaphor for flow, creating digestible abstraction
Arcade/puzzle game aesthetic: straightforward grid presentation without diegetic narrative

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Pipe Dream (NES) article
Wikipedia - Pipe Mania article (covers gameplay mechanics, level design, and progression systems applicable to NES version)
